questions.question: Who created and maintains this export, and what upstream document produced it?
questions.answer: RYDN means Ryden. The CSV was downloaded from Ryden Systems and is a derivative copy of an asserted authoritative Star Atlas ship-stat document.
questions.remaining_gap: The upstream Star Atlas document URL and version are UNKNOWN, and the lineage has not been independently verified.

questions.question: What collection scope does the export establish?
questions.answer: Normalize only the 63 base-ship rows included in the 2026-07-18 Ryden export; do not assert that the collection is complete or currently available.
questions.remaining_gap: Collection completeness and current-availability scope are UNKNOWN.

questions.question: How should the values be scoped across game modules and future versions?
questions.answer: Treat the captured values as base-ship values intended to remain common across SAGE and C4, with future rebasing possible. Holosim uses a different value system and is out of scope. Future components may modify individual ship-instance stats; no hypothetical modifiers are applied here.
questions.remaining_gap: Future rebases and component-level instance effects require separate versioned data when available.

questions.question: How should the unnamed first-column codes be modeled?
questions.answer: Preserve each source-supplied ship shorthand code exactly. The codes usually align with marketplace ship IDs, but exact equivalence is not established.
questions.remaining_gap: Marketplace ID alignment remains unverified per row.

questions.question: Should missing marketplace hyperlinks be recovered or invented?
questions.answer: Keep marketplace hyperlinks missing and unrecovered. Do not invent URLs.
questions.remaining_gap: Marketplace URLs remain unavailable in the supplied artifact.

questions.question: How should the three always-identical semantically unknown metrics be handled?
questions.answer: Omit USDC/Cargo Capacity, USDC/Mining Rate, and Cargo/Warp Fuel from the concise normalized record. Preserve their exact values in the immutable raw CSV and document each omission in the field-disposition ledger.
questions.remaining_gap: The source meaning and derivation of all three omitted metrics remain UNKNOWN.

questions.question: Which units and scales are resolved?
questions.answer: Warp speed is literal and fixed at 100000 astronomical units per second for all 63 ships; warp cooldowns vary by ship. Preserve every other unit exactly as captured without inventing scale definitions.
questions.remaining_gap: Native scale definitions for capacities, consumables, SDU, and other movement values remain unresolved.

questions.question: Should captured names and specialization labels be corrected?
questions.answer: Preserve ship names and specialization labels exactly as captured; make no spelling or capitalization corrections.

research_gaps.description: Upstream Star Atlas document URL/version and independent lineage verification remain unavailable.
The export does not establish completeness or current availability beyond its 63 included rows.
Marketplace IDs and URLs are missing; shorthand-code alignment is unverified per row.
Units/scales other than the explicitly resolved warp speed/AU context remain as captured and require future documentation.
